title: EU Nears Approval of €90 Billion ($106B) Loan Package for Ukraine
detail: The European Union moved toward approving a €90 billion ($106B) loan package to cover Ukraine's military and financial needs for two years. The diplomatic breakthrough came following the April 12 electoral defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán — long the primary EU vetoist on Ukraine aid — by pro-European opposition leader Péter Magyar. With Hungary's blocking position removed, EU members reached broad consensus on the largest-ever single financial commitment to Ukraine. The package is designed to fund defense procurement, reconstruction, and budget support.

media.caption: EU moves to approve $106 billion loan package for Ukraine after Hungary's aid blockade lifted

title: Kremlin Says Putin Ready for 'Result-Oriented' Zelensky Meeting
detail: Russian Presidential Press Secretary Dmitry Peskov stated that Putin is ready to meet with Zelensky but only for 'result-oriented' negotiations when agreements are being finalized. Peskov claimed Moscow sees 'no political will' from Kyiv. The statement came amid Zelensky's active push for direct talks — he had offered to meet Putin in Istanbul, the Middle East, or the United States, and repeated the offer during an Azerbaijan visit on April 25. The diplomatic posturing reflected both sides testing the other's willingness to negotiate under US pressure.

media.caption: Kremlin's Peskov says Putin ready for result-oriented talks with Zelensky
